what is java: Java is a powerful, object-oriented programming language used for building large-scale applications. It runs on the Java Virtual Machine (JVM), making it platform-independent. Created by Sun Microsystems.
what is c++: C++ is a high-performance programming language used for system software, games, and performance-critical applications. It's known for its speed and efficiency.
what is ruby: Ruby is a dynamic, high-level programming language known for being elegant and user-friendly. It's widely used in web development with frameworks like Ruby on Rails.
what is golang: Go (Golang) is a programming language developed by Google. It's designed for simplicity, efficiency, and is popular for cloud computing and microservices.
what is rust: Rust is a systems programming language focused on memory safety and performance. It prevents many common bugs and is used for system software and performance-critical applications.
what is ai: AI (Artificial Intelligence) is technology that enables computers to learn from data and perform tasks that typically require human intelligence, such as decision-making, language understanding, and image recognition.
what is machine learning: Machine Learning is a subset of AI where computers learn patterns from data and improve their performance without explicit programming. It powers recommendation systems, image recognition, and more.
what is deep learning: Deep Learning is a subset of Machine Learning using neural networks with multiple layers. It's particularly effective for image recognition, natural language processing, and complex pattern recognition.
what is nlp: NLP (Natural Language Processing) is a field of AI that focuses on enabling computers to understand and process human language. It powers chatbots, translation services, and sentiment analysis.
what is computer vision: Computer Vision is a field of AI focused on enabling computers to interpret and analyze visual information from images and videos, similar to human vision.
what is html: HTML is the standard markup language for creating web pages. It provides the structure and content of websites. HTML stands for HyperText Markup Language.
html: HTML is the markup language used to create web pages. It provides structure and content using tags and elements.
what is css: CSS is used for styling web pages - controlling colors, fonts, layouts, and visual design. CSS stands for Cascading Style Sheets.
css: CSS is a styling language that controls the appearance of web pages including colors, fonts, and layout.
what is nodejs: Node.js is a JavaScript runtime that lets you run JavaScript outside the browser, typically on servers. It's used for building fast, scalable network applications.
nodejs: Node.js is a JavaScript runtime for server-side development. It's popular for building web applications, APIs, and real-time applications.
what is react: React is a JavaScript library for building dynamic user interfaces with reusable components. It was developed by Facebook and is widely used in web development.
react: React is a JavaScript library for building interactive user interfaces using components. It's maintained by Facebook and is one of the most popular frontend libraries.
what is vue: Vue.js is a progressive JavaScript framework for building user interfaces. It's known for being beginner-friendly and has great documentation.
what is angular: Angular is a comprehensive JavaScript framework for building single-page applications. It's maintained by Google and includes built-in tools for routing, HTTP, and forms.
what is api: APIs (Application Programming Interfaces) enable software components to communicate. REST APIs, GraphQL, and SOAP are common architectures.
api: An API (Application Programming Interface) enables different software systems to communicate and share data.
what is database: A database is an organized collection of data stored electronically. Common databases include MySQL, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, SQLite, and Oracle.
database: A database is an organized collection of structured data. Popular databases include MySQL, PostgreSQL, MongoDB, and SQLite.
what is sql: SQL (Structured Query Language) is used to manage and query relational databases. It allows you to retrieve, insert, update, and delete data.
what is nosql: NoSQL databases are non-relational databases designed for flexibility and scalability. Examples include MongoDB, Redis, Cassandra, and DynamoDB.
what is blockchain: Blockchain is a distributed ledger technology that records transactions across multiple computers securely. It's the foundation of cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin and Ethereum.
blockchain: Blockchain is a distributed ledger technology that records data across many computers. It's known for security and transparency.
what is cloud computing: Cloud computing is the use of remote servers (usually accessed via the internet) to store and process data instead of using local computers. Examples include AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud.
cloud computing: Cloud computing provides computing services over the internet. Major providers include AWS, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud.
what is cybersecurity: Cybersecurity is protecting systems, networks, and data from digital attacks. It includes firewalls, encryption, authentication, and threat detection.
cybersecurity: Cybersecurity involves protecting systems and data from digital attacks and unauthorized access.
what is iot: IoT (Internet of Things) refers to physical devices embedded with sensors and software that connect to the internet to exchange data.
iot: IoT (Internet of Things) connects physical devices to the internet for data exchange and automation.
what is big data: Big Data refers to extremely large datasets that can be analyzed to reveal patterns and insights beyond human analysis.
big data: Big Data involves analyzing large datasets for insights. It requires tools for processing, storage, and analysis.
what is devops: DevOps is a set of practices that combines software development and IT operations to shorten development cycles and improve quality.
devops: DevOps combines development and operations for faster, more reliable software delivery.
what is microservices: Microservices are a software architecture approach where applications are built as loosely coupled, independently deployable small services.
microservices: Microservices architecture breaks applications into small, independent services for better scalability.
what is docker: Docker is a containerization platform that packages applications and their dependencies into containers for easy deployment.
docker: Docker is a containerization tool that packages applications with their dependencies for consistent deployment.
what is kubernetes: Kubernetes is an open-source orchestration platform for automating deployment, scaling, and management of containerized applications.
kubernetes: Kubernetes automates deployment and management of containerized applications at scale.
what is web development: Web development is the process of creating and maintaining websites using HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and other technologies.
web development: Web development involves creating websites and web applications using various technologies and frameworks.
what is mobile development: Mobile development is creating applications for smartphones and tablets using languages like Java, Swift, Kotlin, React Native, or Flutter.
what is testing: Testing is the process of evaluating software to ensure it works as intended, is free from bugs, and meets requirements.
what is git: Git is a version control system that tracks changes in code, allowing developers to collaborate and manage project history.
git: Git is a version control system for tracking code changes and enabling team collaboration.
what is github: GitHub is a platform for version control using Git enabling collaboration on code. It's essential for open-source development and team coding.
github: GitHub is a platform for hosting and collaborating on code repositories using Git version control.
what is agile: Agile is a software development methodology focused on iterative development, continuous feedback, and flexibility to change requirements.
what is rest api: REST API is an architectural style for building web services that use HTTP requests to perform operations on resources.
what is india: India is a country in South Asia with a population of over 1.4 billion people. It's the world's largest democracy with a rich culture and history spanning thousands of years. Capital: New Delhi.
india: India is the world's largest democracy with over 1.4 billion people. It's known for its diverse culture, history, and tech industry.
capital of india: New Delhi is the capital of India. It's located in northern India and serves as the seat of the Indian government.
new delhi: New Delhi is the capital city of India, located in northern India. It's the seat of the Indian government and a major political and cultural center.
what is usa: The USA (United States of America) is a North American country with 50 states. Washington D.C. is its capital. It's the world's largest economy and a major global superpower.
usa: The United States is a North American country with 50 states. Washington D.C. is its capital. It's the world's largest economy.
what is china: China is the most populous country in the world with over 1.4 billion people. Beijing is its capital. It's a major economic and political power with a history spanning thousands of years.
china: China is the world's most populous country with over 1.4 billion people. Beijing is its capital. It's an economic powerhouse.
what is japan: Japan is an island nation in East Asia known for its technology, culture, anime, and innovation. Tokyo is its capital. It's the third-largest economy in the world.
japan: Japan is an island nation in East Asia known for technology, culture, and innovation. Tokyo is its capital.
what is london: London is the capital of the United Kingdom and the largest city in Europe. It's a major financial, cultural, and political center with rich history.
london: London is the capital of the UK and one of the world's largest cities. It's a major financial and cultural hub.
what is paris: Paris is the capital of France and is known as 'The City of Light'. It's famous for the Eiffel Tower, art, cuisine, and culture. It's a major tourist destination.
paris: Paris is the capital of France, famous for the Eiffel Tower, art, and culture. It's one of the world's most visited cities.
what is new york: New York is the most populous city in the United States. It's known as the 'City That Never Sleeps' and is a global financial, cultural, and entertainment center.
new york: New York is the most populous US city, known for Wall Street, Broadway, and diverse culture.
what is gravity: Gravity is a fundamental force of nature that attracts objects with mass toward each other. It's described by Newton's law of universal gravitation and Einstein's general relativity theory.
gravity: Gravity is the force that attracts objects with mass. It keeps planets in orbit and keeps us on Earth.
what is evolution: Evolution is the process by which living organisms change and adapt over generations through natural selection. It's the foundation of modern biology, proposed by Charles Darwin.
evolution: Evolution is the process by which organisms change and adapt over time through natural selection. It's the basis of modern biology.
what is photosynthesis: Photosynthesis is the process by which plants convert sunlight into chemical energy using chlorophyll. It produces glucose and oxygen as byproducts and is essential for life on Earth.
photosynthesis: Photosynthesis is how plants convert sunlight into energy. It produces oxygen and glucose, essential for life.
what is dna: DNA (Deoxyribonucleic Acid) is a molecule that carries genetic instructions for life. It's found in the cells of all living organisms and contains the blueprint for heredity.
dna: DNA (Deoxyribonucleic Acid) carries genetic information in living organisms. It determines traits and heredity.
what is the periodic table: The periodic table is an organized arrangement of all known chemical elements. It arranges elements by atomic number and chemical properties.
what is physics: Physics is the science that studies matter, energy, and forces. It includes mechanics, thermodynamics, electromagnetism, and quantum mechanics.
what is chemistry: Chemistry is the science that studies the properties of substances and how they interact. It includes organic, inorganic, and physical chemistry.
what is biology: Biology is the science that studies living organisms and life processes. It includes genetics, ecology, evolution, and physiology.
who is gandhi: Mahatma Gandhi was an Indian independence activist who led India's freedom struggle against British rule through non-violent resistance and civil disobedience. He was a major political and spiritual leader.
gandhi: Mahatma Gandhi led India's independence movement through non-violence. He's a global symbol of peace and resistance.
who is einstein: Albert Einstein was a theoretical physicist who developed the theory of relativity. He won the Nobel Prize in Physics and is considered one of the greatest scientists of all time.
einstein: Albert Einstein developed the theory of relativity and is one of history's greatest scientists.
who is newton: Isaac Newton was an English mathematician and physicist who formulated the laws of motion and universal gravitation. He's considered one of the most influential scientists ever.
newton: Isaac Newton formulated laws of motion and gravity. He's one of the most influential scientists in history.
what is world war 2: World War II (1939-1945) involved Axis (Germany, Japan, Italy) vs. Allies (USA, UK, USSR). It killed 70+ million, resulted in Holocaust, and created nuclear weapons.
what is the renaissance: The Renaissance (14th-17th centuries) was European cultural revival emphasizing humanism and classical learning. It marked the transition to modernity.
what is the industrial revolution: The Industrial Revolution was a period of major human economic and social change from the 18th to 19th centuries, marked by the shift from agricultural to industrial societies.
who is cristiano ronaldo: Cristiano Ronaldo is a Portuguese footballer and one of greatest ever. He won 5 Ballon d'Or awards and numerous titles.
ronaldo: Cristiano Ronaldo is a legendary Portuguese footballer, one of the greatest players ever. He's won multiple Ballon d'Or awards.
who is messi: Lionel Messi is an Argentine footballer considered one of the greatest of all time. He won the FIFA World Cup with Argentina in 2022 and has won 7 Ballon d'Or awards.
messi: Lionel Messi is an Argentine footballer, widely considered the greatest of all time. He won the 2022 World Cup.
who is shahrukh khan: Shah Rukh Khan is an Indian film actor and producer, often called 'King of Bollywood'. He's one of the most popular and influential actors in Indian cinema.
shahrukh khan: Shah Rukh Khan is a legendary Bollywood actor known for romantic films and is one of the most popular actors globally.
who is taylor swift: Taylor Swift is an American singer-songwriter known for writing catchy pop and country songs. She's one of the best-selling music artists globally with multiple Grammy Awards.
taylor swift: Taylor Swift is an American singer-songwriter and one of the best-selling music artists. She's known for her songwriting and chart-topping albums.
who is oprah winfrey: Oprah Winfrey is a media mogul and former talk show host. She built media empire and became billionaire through entrepreneurship.
oprah: Oprah Winfrey is a media mogul, talk show host, and philanthropist. She's one of the most influential women in the world.
who is elon musk: Elon Musk is a South African-born entrepreneur and CEO of Tesla and SpaceX. He's one of the world's richest people and a visionary in electric vehicles and space exploration.
elon musk: Elon Musk is CEO of Tesla and SpaceX. He's a tech entrepreneur focused on electric vehicles and space exploration.
what is stock market: The stock market is a place where shares of publicly-held companies are traded. It's where investors buy and sell stocks to own portions of companies.
what is cryptocurrency: Cryptocurrency is a digital currency that uses cryptography for security. Bitcoin and Ethereum are the most well-known cryptocurrencies.
what is bitcoin: Bitcoin is a decentralized digital currency and the first cryptocurrency. It was created in 2009 and operates on blockchain technology without central authority.
bitcoin: Bitcoin is a digital currency created in 2009. It operates on blockchain technology and is decentralized.
what is ethereum: Ethereum is a blockchain platform that enables smart contracts and decentralized applications. It's the second-largest cryptocurrency by market cap.
ethereum: Ethereum is a blockchain platform for smart contracts and decentralized apps. It's the second-largest cryptocurrency.
what is nft: NFT (Non-Fungible Token) is a unique digital asset verified on blockchain. It represents ownership of digital items like art, collectibles, and media.
nft: NFT (Non-Fungible Token) is a unique digital asset on blockchain representing ownership of digital items.
what is inflation: Inflation is the general increase in prices of goods and services over time, reducing purchasing power. It's a key economic indicator.
what is gdp: GDP (Gross Domestic Product) measures the total monetary value of goods and services produced by a country in a specific period.
what is yoga: Yoga is an ancient Indian practice combining physical postures, breathing techniques, and meditation. It's known for improving flexibility, strength, and mental well-being.
yoga: Yoga is an ancient practice combining physical exercises, breathing, and meditation for health and wellness.
what is meditation: Meditation is a mental practice involving focused attention and mindfulness. It's used for stress reduction, mental clarity, and emotional well-being.
meditation: Meditation is a practice for mental health involving focused attention and mindfulness.
what is exercise: Exercise is physical activity performed to improve health, fitness, and well-being. It includes activities like running, swimming, weightlifting, and sports.
what is nutrition: Nutrition is the science of food and its effects on health. A balanced diet includes proteins, carbohydrates, fats, vitamins, and minerals.
nutrition: Nutrition involves consuming balanced foods for health. It includes proteins, carbohydrates, fats, vitamins, and minerals.
what is a degree: A degree is a qualification awarded by a college or university after completing academic studies. Examples include Bachelor's, Master's, and PhD degrees.
what is machine learning certification: Machine Learning certification demonstrates expertise in ML concepts, algorithms, and tools. Popular certifications include those from Coursera, Google, and Andrew Ng.
what time is it: I don't have real-time access, but you can check the time on your device's clock.
what is love: Love is a deep feeling of affection and attachment toward someone or something. It's a fundamental human emotion.
what is life: Life is the existence of organisms and the quality of being alive. It's characterized by growth, metabolism, reproduction, and response to environment.
what is the meaning of life: The meaning of life is a philosophical question. Different cultures and individuals have different answers - some find it in family, achievement, love, spirituality, or helping others.
why do we exist: Why we exist is a philosophical question. Answers vary from religious beliefs to scientific perspectives. Many people find purpose through relationships, creativity, and helping others.
what is typescript: TypeScript is a superset of JavaScript adding static typing. It helps catch errors early and improves code maintainability for large projects.
what is scala: Scala is a hybrid functional and object-oriented language running on the JVM. It's popular for big data processing with frameworks like Apache Spark.
what is kotlin: Kotlin is a modern language for Android development and the JVM. It's more concise and safer than Java while maintaining full interoperability.
what is swift: Swift is Apple's programming language for iOS, macOS, and other Apple platforms. It's faster, safer, and more expressive than Objective-C.
what is csharp: C# is a modern object-oriented language developed by Microsoft for .NET framework. It's used for Windows, web, mobile, and game development.
what is webassembly: WebAssembly is a low-level binary format for browsers enabling high-performance applications. It allows languages like C++, Rust to run in browsers.
what is graphql: GraphQL is a query language for APIs providing precise data fetching. It allows clients to request exactly what data they need, improving efficiency.
what is nosql database: NoSQL databases store data without fixed schemas. Types include document (MongoDB), key-value (Redis), column-family (Cassandra), and graph databases.
what is redis: Redis is an in-memory data structure store used for caching and real-time applications. It's extremely fast and supports various data types.
what is mongodb: MongoDB is a document-oriented NoSQL database storing JSON-like documents. It's flexible and scalable for modern applications.
what is elasticsearch: Elasticsearch is a search and analytics engine built on Lucene. It enables full-text search, log analysis, and real-time data insights.
what is apache kafka: Apache Kafka is a distributed streaming platform for real-time data pipelines. It handles high-throughput, fault-tolerant data processing.
what is tensorflow: TensorFlow is Google's open-source machine learning framework. It enables building and training deep learning models at scale.
what is pytorch: PyTorch is a machine learning library developed by Meta for deep learning. It's known for flexibility and ease of use in research and production.
what is sklearn: scikit-learn is a Python library for machine learning providing classical algorithms. It's widely used for classification, regression, and clustering.
what is numpy: NumPy is a Python library for numerical computing providing arrays and mathematical functions. It's the foundation for scientific Python.
what is pandas: Pandas is a Python library for data manipulation and analysis. It provides dataframes for handling structured data efficiently.
what is aws: AWS (Amazon Web Services) is the largest cloud provider offering 200+ services. It enables businesses to scale computationally without physical infrastructure.
what is azure: Azure is Microsoft's cloud platform providing computing, storage, and analytics services. It integrates well with Microsoft products and enterprise tools.
what is gcp: Google Cloud Platform offers cloud computing services including compute, storage, databases, and analytics. It's known for data analytics and AI/ML capabilities.
what is heroku: Heroku is a cloud platform enabling easy deployment of web applications. It abstracts infrastructure complexity, ideal for startups and rapid prototyping.
what is figma: Figma is a cloud-based design tool for UI/UX design and prototyping. It enables real-time collaboration among design teams globally.
what is jira: Jira is project management software from Atlassian used for agile teams. It tracks issues, sprints, and projects enabling organized development.
what is slack: Slack is a communication platform for teams enabling chat, file sharing, and integrations. It's become essential for remote work communication.
what is notion: Notion is an all-in-one workspace combining notes, databases, wikis, and kanban boards. It's used for personal and team organization.
what is gitlab: GitLab is a DevOps platform providing Git hosting, CI/CD, and project management. It can be self-hosted or cloud-based.
what is jenkins: Jenkins is an open-source automation server for continuous integration/deployment. It automates building, testing, and deploying applications.
what is selenium: Selenium is a tool for automating web browser testing. It enables automated UI testing across browsers and platforms.
what is web3: Web3 is the next iteration of the internet using blockchain, cryptocurrencies, and decentralization. It aims to give users ownership and control of data.
what is defi: DeFi (Decentralized Finance) uses blockchain for financial services without intermediaries. It enables lending, trading, and financial services peer-to-peer.
what is metaverse: The metaverse is proposed interconnected virtual/augmented worlds where people interact, work, and play. It combines AR/VR, AI, and blockchain.
what is quantum internet: Quantum internet uses quantum mechanics for unhackable, ultra-fast communication. It's emerging technology that could revolutionize connectivity.
what is 6g: 6G is next-generation wireless after 5G promising terabit speeds. It's in research phase but could enable metaverse and holographic communication.
what is cpu: A CPU (Central Processing Unit) is the brain of a computer executing instructions. Modern CPUs have multiple cores for parallel processing.
what is gpu: A GPU (Graphics Processing Unit) accelerates graphics and parallel computing. GPUs power deep learning, gaming, and scientific computing.
what is quantum processor: Quantum processors use qubits for quantum computing potentially exponentially faster than classical computers for specific problems.
what is tpu: TPU (Tensor Processing Unit) is Google's AI-specific processor optimized for machine learning. It's faster and more efficient than GPUs for neural networks.
what is semiconductor: Semiconductors are materials enabling transistor creation, the basis of all electronics. Chips like those from TSMC power all modern devices.
what is Moore's law: Moore's Law states transistor count on chips doubles every 2 years, enabling exponential computing power increases. It's slowing but still holds approximately.
what is zero trust security: Zero Trust Security assumes all access is untrusted until verified. It requires authentication for every user and device, improving security.
what is penetration testing: Penetration testing is authorized hacking to find security vulnerabilities. Ethical hackers simulate attacks to identify and fix security weaknesses.
what is privacy: Privacy is the right to control one's personal information and online activity. It's increasingly important as data collection expands.
what is gdpr: GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) is EU regulation protecting personal data. It gives users control of data and holds companies accountable.
what is ccpa: CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act) gives California residents data privacy rights. It's one of the strongest privacy laws in the US.
what is digital signature: Digital signatures use cryptography to authenticate documents electronically. They prove origin and integrity of digital communications.
what is two factor authentication: Two-factor authentication (2FA) requires two verification methods like password + phone code. It significantly improves account security.
what is biometric: Biometrics use biological traits like fingerprints, face, or iris for authentication. They're convenient and harder to forge than passwords.
what is pandemic: A pandemic is a disease outbreak spread across wide geographic areas affecting large populations. COVID-19 is a recent example with global impact.
what is covid-19: COVID-19 is a respiratory disease caused by SARS-CoV-2 virus. It emerged in 2019, spread globally, killing millions, and causing societal disruption.
what is vaccine: A vaccine is a biological preparation providing immunity to disease. It contains weakened/inactive pathogens stimulating immune response.
what is mrna vaccine: mRNA vaccines teach cells to make viral proteins triggering immune response. Pfizer and Moderna COVID vaccines are mRNA vaccines.
what is immune system: The immune system protects the body from pathogens like bacteria and viruses. It includes white blood cells, antibodies, and lymph nodes.
what is metabolism: Metabolism is the set of chemical reactions keeping organisms alive. It converts nutrients to energy and produces waste.
what is genetics: Genetics studies inheritance and variation of traits through genes. It explains how organisms inherit characteristics from parents.
what is gene therapy: Gene therapy introduces genes to cure genetic diseases or boost immunity. It's emerging treatment showing promise for inherited disorders.
what is crispr: CRISPR is a gene-editing tool enabling precise DNA modifications. It could cure genetic diseases, enhance crops, and extend human lifespan.
what is stem cell: Stem cells are undifferentiated cells that can become specialized cells. They could repair damaged tissue and treat diseases.
what is cloning: Cloning creates genetically identical organisms. Therapeutic cloning produces tissues for medicine; reproductive cloning creates organisms.
what is supercomputer: Supercomputers are fastest computers performing trillions of calculations per second. They're used for simulations, AI training, and scientific research.
what is petaflop: A petaflop is one quadrillion floating-point calculations per second. Modern supercomputers achieve petaflop and exaflop performance.
what is data science: Data science combines statistics, programming, and domain expertise to extract insights from data. It's fundamental to modern decision-making.
what is analytics: Analytics is analyzing data to find patterns and insights. Descriptive analytics shows what happened; predictive analytics forecasts future.
what is business intelligence: Business intelligence is technology enabling data-driven decisions. It includes analytics, dashboards, and reporting tools.
what is market research: Market research is gathering information about customers, competitors, and markets. It informs business strategy and product development.
what is brand: A brand is a company's identity, reputation, and promise. Strong brands command loyalty and premium pricing.
what is marketing: Marketing is promoting and selling products/services. It includes advertising, social media, content creation, and customer engagement.
what is customer experience: Customer experience is all interactions a customer has with a brand. Good CX increases loyalty, retention, and lifetime value.
what is supply chain: Supply chain is the process of delivering products from producer to consumer. It includes sourcing, manufacturing, distribution, and sales.
what is logistics: Logistics involves planning and executing movement and storage of goods. It ensures products reach right place at right time cost-effectively.
what is stoicism: Stoicism is an ancient philosophy teaching virtue is highest good, emotions should be controlled, and living in harmony with reason/nature. Zeno founded it in Athens.
what is epicureanism: Epicureanism advocates simple living and intellectual pleasures over excess. Despite misconceptions, it emphasizes moderation and friendship.
what is taoism: Taoism is an ancient Chinese philosophy and religion emphasizing the Tao, the natural way of the universe.
what is confucianism: Confucianism is Chinese philosophy emphasizing virtue, respect, and hierarchical relationships. Founded by Confucius, it shaped East Asian culture.
what is buddhism: Buddhism is a spiritual tradition founded by Siddhartha Gautama (Buddha). It teaches the path to enlightenment through meditation and ethical living.
what is zen buddhism: Zen Buddhism emphasizes meditation and intuitive insight over doctrine. It originated in China and is influential in Japan.
what is daoism: Daoism (Taoism) values harmonizing with the Tao, simplicity, and naturalness. It influenced Chinese culture, medicine, and martial arts.
lao tzu: Lao Tzu was a Chinese philosopher credited with founding Taoism. He allegedly wrote the Tao Te Ching, a foundational philosophical text.
what is aristotle: Aristotle (384-322 BC) was a Greek philosopher covering logic, metaphysics, ethics, and science. He founded the Lyceum school.
what is roman empire: The Roman Empire (27 BC - 476 AD) was one of history's greatest empires controlling much of Europe, North Africa, and Asia. It shaped Western civilization.
what is feudalism: Feudalism was a medieval social system with lords, vassals, and serfs. It was based on land ownership and mutual obligations for protection.
what is the crusades: The Crusades were medieval military campaigns (1095-1291) by Christians to reclaim Jerusalem from Muslims. They had lasting geopolitical impact.
what is the dark ages: The Dark Ages (5th-10th centuries) was a period of decline after Roman Empire's fall. It featured feudalism, limited literacy, and cultural preservation.
what is leonardo da vinci: Leonardo da Vinci (1452-1519) was an Italian polymath - artist, scientist, engineer. He created Mona Lisa and advanced knowledge in anatomy and physics.
what is michelangelo: Michelangelo (1475-1564) was an Italian Renaissance artist. He painted the Sistine Chapel and sculpted David.
what is the printing press: The printing press, invented by Gutenberg (~1440), revolutionized communication enabling mass production of books. It enabled the Renaissance and Reformation.
what is gutenberg: Johannes Gutenberg invented the movable-type printing press around 1440. It revolutionized communication, knowledge sharing, and led to the Renaissance.
what is the age of exploration: The Age of Exploration (15th-17th centuries) saw European exploration of new lands. Columbus reached Americas (1492), Vasco da Gama reached India, Magellan circumnavigated globe.
what is christopher columbus: Christopher Columbus was an Italian explorer sailing for Spain. In 1492, he sailed to the Caribbean, initiating European colonization of the Americas.
what is world war 1: World War I (1914-1918) involved major nations in devastating trench warfare. It killed millions, led to Russian Revolution, and created political instability.
what is the holocaust: The Holocaust was Nazi Germany's genocide of 6 million Jews (1941-1945). It's history's most documented atrocity and led to 'Never Again' movements.
what is hitler: Adolf Hitler was Nazi Germany's dictator (1934-1945). He initiated WWII and Holocaust, resulting in millions of deaths before his suicide.
what is the cold war: The Cold War (1947-1991) was ideological conflict between USA (capitalism) and USSR (communism). It featured proxy wars, nuclear threat, and space race without direct military conflict.
what is the space race: The Space Race (1957-1975) was competition between USA and USSR for space supremacy. USSR launched Sputnik and first human in space; USA landed on Moon (1969).
what is the moon landing: The Apollo 11 Moon landing (July 20, 1969) was humanity's greatest achievement. Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin became first humans on the Moon.
what is neil armstrong: Neil Armstrong was an American astronaut who became the first person to walk on the Moon on July 20, 1969. He spoke: 'That's one small step for man...'
who is churchill: Winston Churchill was British PM (1940-1945, 1951-1955) during WWII. He's famous for 'Never surrender' speeches and defying Nazi Germany.
who is abraham lincoln: Abraham Lincoln was US President (1861-1865) during the Civil War. He abolished slavery but was assassinated.
what is the american civil war: The American Civil War (1861-1865) was fought between Union (North) and Confederacy (South). It ended slavery and kept the USA united.
what is mlk: Martin Luther King Jr. was an American civil rights leader (1929-1968). He led non-violent movement against racial segregation and was assassinated.
what is rosa parks: Rosa Parks was an American civil rights activist. Her refusal to give up her bus seat in 1955 sparked the Montgomery bus boycott against segregation.
what is nelson mandela: Nelson Mandela was a South African anti-apartheid activist and president (1994-1999). He fought racism, spent 27 years in prison, and became a global symbol of freedom.
who is napoleon: Napoleon Bonaparte was a French general and emperor (1799-1814). He conquered much of Europe before being defeated and exiled.
what is indira gandhi: Indira Gandhi was India's PM (1966-77, 1980-84) and daughter of Jawaharlal Nehru. She modernized India but was assassinated.
what is margaret thatcher: Margaret Thatcher was British PM (1979-1990). She's known for conservative economics (Thatcherism) and strong leadership.
what is singularity: Technological Singularity is a hypothetical point when AI becomes superintelligent surpassing human intelligence. It could transform civilization or pose existential risk.
what is agi: Artificial General Intelligence (AGI) is AI matching or exceeding human intelligence across all domains. Unlike narrow AI, AGI would be versatile and self-improving.
what is neural network: Neural networks are computing systems inspired by biological brains. They're used in deep learning for image recognition, language processing, and AI.
what is transformer: Transformers are neural network architectures using attention mechanisms. They power large language models like GPT and are foundation of modern NLP.
what is bert: BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers) is Google's NLP model. It understands context and has advanced language understanding.
what is gpt: GPT (Generative Pre-trained Transformer) is an AI language model by OpenAI. It generates human-like text for conversations, writing, and coding.
what is chatgpt: ChatGPT is an AI chatbot by OpenAI based on GPT. It has conversational abilities and can answer questions, write, and code.
what is large language model: Large Language Models (LLMs) are neural networks trained on massive text data. They can generate text, translate, summarize, and answer questions.
what is image generation ai: Image generation AI like DALL-E creates images from text descriptions. It uses deep learning to synthesize novel images matching descriptions.
what is reinforcement learning: Reinforcement learning is ML where agents learn through trial-and-error with rewards/penalties. It powers game-playing AI like AlphaGo.
what is supervised learning: Supervised learning trains models on labeled data with known outputs. It's used for classification and regression tasks.
what is unsupervised learning: Unsupervised learning finds patterns in unlabeled data. It's used for clustering, dimensionality reduction, and anomaly detection.
what is transfer learning: Transfer learning uses knowledge from one task to solve another. Pre-trained models can be fine-tuned for new tasks efficiently.
what is rest: REST (Representational State Transfer) is an API architectural style using HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) to manage resources.
what is soap: SOAP is an older XML-based protocol for web services. It's more complex than REST but provides strong typing and security.
what is middleware: Middleware is software between OS and applications. In web, it processes requests/responses (authentication, logging, compression).
what is load balancing: Load balancing distributes network traffic across multiple servers. It improves performance, reliability, and prevents overload.
what is caching: Caching stores frequently accessed data for faster retrieval. It reduces database load and improves application performance.
what is cdn: CDN (Content Delivery Network) distributes content geographically. It speeds up delivery by serving from nearest servers.
what is virtualization: Virtualization creates virtual computers/servers on physical hardware. It enables efficient resource usage and isolation.
what is containerization: Containerization packages applications with dependencies (Docker). Containers are lightweight, portable, and consistent across environments.
what is orm: ORM (Object-Relational Mapping) maps database tables to code objects. It abstracts SQL, simplifying database interactions.
what is orm framework: ORM frameworks like Hibernate, SQLAlchemy map objects to databases. They reduce SQL writing and improve maintainability.
what is serverless: Serverless computing runs code without managing servers. AWS Lambda, Google Cloud Functions execute code on-demand.
what is faas: FaaS (Function as a Service) lets developers write functions responding to events. AWS Lambda is a popular FaaS platform.
what is paas: PaaS (Platform as a Service) provides development platforms. Developers build apps without managing underlying infrastructure.
what is iaas: IaaS (Infrastructure as a Service) provides computing resources (compute, storage, networking). AWS, Azure, GCP are IaaS providers.
what is saas: SaaS (Software as a Service) delivers applications over internet (Gmail, Salesforce, Slack). Users access via browser without installation.
what is fusion energy: Fusion energy combines atoms (like sun) producing massive energy. It's clean, abundant, and could solve energy crisis (commercialization delayed past 2050).
what is renewable energy: Renewable energy comes from natural resources (sun, wind, water). Solar, wind, hydroelectric power are becoming dominant energy sources.
what is climate change: Climate change is long-term shift in temperatures/patterns from greenhouse gases. It causes extreme weather, sea-level rise, and ecosystem disruption.
what is electric vehicles: Electric vehicles use batteries instead of gasoline. They produce zero emissions, have lower running costs, and are rapidly expanding.
what is autonomous vehicles: Autonomous vehicles drive without human intervention using sensors/AI. They could reduce accidents, transform transportation, and change cities.
what is self driving car: Self-driving cars use AI and sensors to navigate autonomously. Companies like Tesla, Waymo develop fully autonomous vehicles.
what is hyperloop: Hyperloop is proposed transportation using pods in low-pressure tubes. It could provide ultra-fast ground transport (700+ mph).
what is 5g: 5G is fifth-generation wireless standard offering gigabit speeds and low latency. It enables IoT, autonomous vehicles, and advanced applications.
what is augmented reality: Augmented Reality overlays digital information on physical world. AR glasses could replace screens and transform how we interact.
what is virtual reality: Virtual Reality creates immersive digital environments. VR headsets transport users to virtual worlds for gaming, training, and social interaction.
what is mixed reality: Mixed Reality combines AR and VR enabling digital objects to interact with physical world. It's the future of immersive computing.
what is brain computer interface: Brain-Computer Interfaces (BCIs) connect brains to computers. They could enable telepathy-like communication, motor control for disabled people.
what is neuralink: Neuralink is Elon Musk's company developing brain-computer interfaces. They aim to enhance human cognition and treat neurological conditions.
what is genetic engineering: Genetic engineering modifies DNA to enhance traits or cure diseases. CRISPR enables precise modifications with huge potential and ethical considerations.
what is synthetic biology: Synthetic biology designs new biological systems. It could create custom organisms for medicine, fuel, materials.
what is whole genome sequencing: Whole genome sequencing reads entire DNA code. It identifies genetic mutations causing diseases and enables personalized medicine.
what is personalized medicine: Personalized medicine tailors treatment to individual genetics and biology. It could improve drug effectiveness and reduce side effects.
what is space colonization: Space colonization establishes human settlements beyond Earth. Mars is primary target for colonization in coming decades.
what is mars colony: Mars colonization would establish human presence on Mars. SpaceX's Starship aims for Mars missions; challenges include life support and resources.
what is asteroid mining: Asteroid mining extracts valuable metals/water from asteroids. It could provide resources and reduce Earth mining environmental impact.
what is doomsday scenarios: Doomsday scenarios include nuclear war, asteroid impact, pandemic, AI risk. Preparedness and prevention are critical for humanity's future.
what is existential risk: Existential risks could end human civilization (nuclear war, AI misalignment, pandemics). Reducing them is crucial for long-term survival.
what is shakespeare: William Shakespeare (1564-1616) was an English playwright and poet. He wrote 37 plays and 154 sonnets including Hamlet, Macbeth, Romeo and Juliet.
what is dante: Dante Alighieri (1265-1321) was an Italian poet. He wrote The Divine Comedy, an epic poem about journey through Hell, Purgatory, and Paradise.
what is jane austen: Jane Austen (1775-1817) was an English novelist. She wrote Pride and Prejudice, Sense and Sensibility exploring romance and social satire.
what is leo tolstoy: Leo Tolstoy (1828-1910) was a Russian novelist. He wrote War and Peace and Anna Karenina, epic novels exploring war, love, and society.
what is fyodor dostoevsky: Fyodor Dostoevsky (1821-1881) was a Russian novelist. He wrote Crime and Punishment and The Brothers Karamazov exploring psychology and morality.
what is homer: Homer was an ancient Greek poet (8th century BC). He wrote the Iliad (Trojan War) and Odyssey (epic journey), foundational to Western literature.
what is miguel cervantes: Miguel Cervantes (1547-1616) was a Spanish writer. He wrote Don Quixote, considered first modern novel, satirizing chivalry romances.
what is mozart: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart (1756-1791) was an Austrian composer. He composed 600+ works including 39 symphonies, operas, and concertos.
what is beethoven: Ludwig van Beethoven (1770-1827) was a German composer. He composed 10 symphonies including the famous Ninth Symphony despite becoming deaf.
what is bach: Johann Sebastian Bach (1685-1750) was a German Baroque composer. He composed over 1,000 works including Brandenburg Concertos and Well-Tempered Clavier.
what is chopin: Frédéric Chopin (1810-1849) was a Polish composer. He specialized in piano, composing nocturnes, mazurkas, etudes celebrated for expression and virtuosity.
what is eiffel tower: The Eiffel Tower is a lattice iron monument in Paris built for 1889 World's Fair. It's iconic symbol of Paris and France attracting millions yearly.
what is colosseum: The Colosseum is an ancient Roman amphitheater in Rome. Built 72-80 AD, it held gladiator contests and is symbol of Roman engineering.
what is taj mahal: The Taj Mahal is a marble mausoleum in Agra, India built by Mughal emperor Shah Jahan for his wife. It's one of world's most beautiful buildings.
what is statue of liberty: The Statue of Liberty is a colossal copper statue in New York. A gift from France, it symbolizes freedom and was seen by immigrants arriving in USA.
what is big ben: Big Ben is the Great Bell of the Elizabeth Tower at Palace of Westminster in London. The clock tower is iconic symbol of London and UK.
what is inception: Inception is a 2010 science fiction film directed by Christopher Nolan about dream infiltration and corporate espionage. It stars Leonardo DiCaprio.
inception: Inception (2010) directed by Christopher Nolan. A mind-bending heist movie set within layered dream worlds.
tell me about inception: Inception is a sci-fi thriller (2010) directed by Christopher Nolan starring Leonardo DiCaprio, Joseph Gordon-Levitt, Ellen Page. It explores dreams within dreams and reality.
what is the godfather: The Godfather (1972) is a classic crime film directed by Francis Ford Coppola, chronicling the Corleone mafia family and starring Marlon Brando and Al Pacino.
the godfather: The Godfather (1972) directed by Francis Ford Coppola. A seminal mafia drama based on Mario Puzo's novel.
what is naruto: Naruto is a Japanese manga and anime series by Masashi Kishimoto following Naruto Uzumaki, a young ninja seeking recognition and aspiring to become Hokage.
naruto: Naruto is a long-running anime/manga series about ninjas, friendship, and perseverance. It has influenced global anime fandom.
what is one piece: One Piece is a popular manga and anime by Eiichiro Oda following Monkey D. Luffy's pirate crew searching for the ultimate treasure, the One Piece.
one piece: One Piece is a globally popular pirate-adventure anime and manga known for world-building and longevity.
what is simpsons: The Simpsons is an American animated sitcom created by Matt Groening. It satirizes American culture through the Simpson family in Springfield.
the simpsons: The Simpsons is a long-running animated sitcom known for satire, cultural references, and iconic characters like Homer and Bart.
what is attack on titan: Attack on Titan is a dark fantasy manga/anime by Hajime Isayama featuring humanity's struggle against giant humanoid Titans.
attack on titan: Attack on Titan is a critically acclaimed anime/manga known for intense storytelling, plot twists, and themes of survival.
what is friends: Friends is an American sitcom (1994-2004) about six friends living in New York City. It's known for humor, character chemistry, and cultural impact.
friends: Friends is a popular sitcom centered on a group of friends navigating life, relationships, and careers in NYC. It remains culturally influential.
what is breaking bad: Breaking Bad is an American crime drama (2008-2013) created by Vince Gilligan about Walter White, a chemistry teacher turned methamphetamine producer.
breaking bad: Breaking Bad is a critically acclaimed drama exploring morality, crime, and transformation, starring Bryan Cranston and Aaron Paul.
what is venture capital: Venture capital invests in startups with high growth potential. VCs take equity in exchange for funding enabling innovation.
what is private equity: Private equity firms buy companies, improve operations, then sell. They focus on profitability and value creation.
what is startup: A startup is new business pursuing innovative ideas. Startups aim for rapid growth and disruption in industries.
what is unicorn startup: A unicorn startup is private company valued over $1 billion. Tech startups like Uber, Airbnb achieved unicorn status.
what is ipo: IPO (Initial Public Offering) is when private company goes public selling shares. It raises capital and gives founders/investors exit.
what is merger and acquisition: M&A involves combining companies (merger) or one buying another (acquisition). It's used for growth, diversification, or eliminating competitors.
what is valuation: Valuation estimates business worth using methods like P/E ratio, DCF analysis. It determines fair price for buying/selling/investing.
what is roi: ROI (Return on Investment) measures investment profitability. Higher ROI indicates better returns.
what is competitive advantage: Competitive advantage is what makes a business better than competitors. It comes from cost efficiency, brand, innovation, or unique resources.
what is disruption: Disruption creates new market by innovating. Uber disrupted taxis, Netflix disrupted video rental with better technology/service.
what is business model: Business model is how company creates and delivers value. Examples: subscription (Netflix), marketplace (eBay), advertising (Google).
what is lean startup: Lean Startup methodology builds products iteratively with customer feedback. It minimizes waste and speeds time-to-market.
what is growth hacking: Growth hacking uses creative, low-cost strategies for rapid user/revenue growth. Tactics include referrals, virality, A/B testing.
what is product market fit: Product-market fit occurs when product satisfies market needs. Companies with PMF grow rapidly with organic demand.
what is customer acquisition cost: CAC is cost to acquire one customer. Lower CAC relative to lifetime value indicates profitable business model.
what is customer lifetime value: CLV is total profit from customer over lifetime. High CLV companies have sustainable, profitable business models.
what is churn rate: Churn rate is percentage of customers leaving in period. Low churn indicates satisfied customers and healthy business.
what is burn rate: Burn rate is monthly spending by startup. Low burn rate extends runway; understanding burn rate is critical for survival.
what is runway: Runway is months a company can operate with current cash. It determines urgency for fundraising or profitability.
what is equity: Equity represents ownership stake in company. Employees get stock options; investors get shares for capital.
what is dividend: A dividend is payment from company profits to shareholders. It represents passive income from investments.
what is bear market: Bear market is extended period of falling stock prices (20%+ decline). It indicates economic weakness and investor pessimism.
what is bull market: Bull market is extended period of rising stock prices. It indicates economic strength and investor optimism.
what is hedge fund: Hedge funds are private investment funds using diverse strategies. They aim for high returns through leverage and derivatives.
what is mutual fund: Mutual funds pool investor money for diversified portfolios. They're managed professionally and accessible to retail investors.
what is etf: ETF (Exchange-Traded Fund) is fund traded on exchanges like stocks. It offers diversification at lower cost than mutual funds.
what is options trading: Options trading involves contracts to buy/sell assets at future price. It enables leverage but carries high risk.
who is steve jobs: Steve Jobs (1955-2011) co-founded Apple, revolutionizing personal computers and mobile phones. He transformed technology and design philosophy.
who is bill gates: Bill Gates co-founded Microsoft creating dominant software. He's a billionaire philanthropist addressing global health/poverty.
who is mark zuckerberg: Mark Zuckerberg founded Facebook (Meta) at 19. He built world's largest social network with billions of users globally.
who is warren buffett: Warren Buffett is an American businessman and investor known as Oracle of Omaha. He's one of world's richest people via value investing.
who is albert einstein: Albert Einstein (1879-1955) developed theory of relativity revolutionizing physics. E=mc² is his most famous equation.
what is samurai: Samurai were Japanese warrior class following bushido code. They served feudal lords (daimyo) until Japanese modernization.
what is vikings: Vikings were Norse seafarers (8th-11th centuries) exploring, trading, and raiding. They reached North America and influenced European history.
what is silk road: Silk Road was ancient trade network connecting China to Mediterranean. It facilitated exchange of goods, ideas, and cultures.
what is egyptian hieroglyphics: Egyptian hieroglyphics were ancient pictographic writing system. They recorded history on papyrus and monuments for millennia.
what is aztec: The Aztec Empire ruled Mexico (14th-16th centuries). They built Tenochtitlan, developed advanced agriculture, mathematics, astronomy.
what is maya: The Maya civilization flourished in Mesoamerica. They developed writing, mathematics, astronomy, and built monumental cities like Chichen Itza.
what is inca: The Inca Empire ruled South America (13th-16th centuries). They built Machu Picchu and had advanced architecture, engineering.
what is persia: Persia was ancient civilization in Iran. Persian Empire under Darius was one of antiquity's largest, influencing Middle East.
what is mesopotamia: Mesopotamia (Iraq) is ancient region between Tigris-Euphrates. Sumer, Babylon, Assyria developed writing, law, agriculture.
who is serena williams: Serena Williams is an American tennis champion. She won 23 Grand Slam singles titles making her one of greatest athletes ever.
who is usain bolt: Usain Bolt is a Jamaican sprinter and 100m world record holder (9.58s). He's fastest human ever and Olympic legend.
who is pele: Pelé was a Brazilian footballer considered greatest of all time. He won 3 FIFA World Cups (1958, 1962, 1970).
who is diego maradona: Diego Maradona was an Argentine footballer considered greatest. He led Argentina to 1986 World Cup, famous for 'Hand of God' goal.
who is michael jordan: Michael Jordan is widely considered greatest basketball player. He won 6 NBA championships with Chicago Bulls.
who is kobe bryant: Kobe Bryant was an American basketball legend (1978-2020). He won 5 NBA championships with LA Lakers.
who is tiger woods: Tiger Woods is a legendary golfer with 15 major championships. He revolutionized golf and inspired younger generation.
who is roger federer: Roger Federer is a Swiss tennis legend. He won 20 Grand Slam singles titles and 8 Wimbledons.
who is lionel messi: Lionel Messi is an Argentine footballer. He won 8 Ballon d'Or awards and led Argentina to 2022 FIFA World Cup victory.
